related_information___professional_organ.related_information_organizations_belong: I am an active member of several professional organizations that support my work in public librarianship and community programming. These memberships keep me current on best practices, provide training opportunities, and connect me with colleagues across Ohio and the broader library community.
related_information___professional_organ.offices_held_row_1: General member since 2019; active participant in Public Library Association (PLA) division discussion groups (no formal elected office)
related_information___professional_organ.offices_held_row_2: Member since 2020; served as volunteer note-taker for the 2023 Small Libraries Division virtual roundtable
related_information___professional_organ.offices_held_row_3: Member since 2021; contributed as an informal peer reviewer for a colleague’s PLA conference proposal (no official title)
related_information___professional_organ.offices_held_row_4: Member since 2022; participated in virtual interest groups on teen programming (no formal office)

statement_of_interest.explain_why_considered_employment_line_1: I would like to be considered for employment with Sandusky Library because this institution played a formative role in my own childhood and inspired me to pursue librarianship as a career. I grew up in Sandusky and spent countless afternoons in the children’s and teen sections, attending storytimes, summer reading programs, and homework help sessions. Those experiences not only supported my academic success but also gave me a sense of belonging and curiosity that I now strive to pass on to others in my professional work.
statement_of_interest.explain_why_considered_employment_line_2: Professionally, I bring a strong combination of formal education and hands-on public library experience that I believe aligns closely with Sandusky Library’s mission. I hold an MLIS with a concentration in public librarianship and have worked in a mid-sized public library system where I planned and led programs for children, teens, and adults; provided front-line reference and reader’s advisory; and supported circulation, technology help, and community outreach. For example, I developed a weekly after-school homework and reading club for middle school students that consistently drew 15–20 regular attendees and led to measurable increases in their library card usage and circulation of age-appropriate materials.
statement_of_interest.explain_why_considered_employment_line_3: I am particularly interested in Sandusky Library because of its strong reputation for community engagement and its unique role as a historic, downtown institution that serves a diverse population of residents, students, and visitors. I am excited by the opportunity to help design inclusive programs that reflect the needs of Sandusky’s families, seniors, and local schools—for instance, partnering with teachers to align library resources with classroom assignments, offering technology basics workshops for adults who are applying for jobs online, and creating welcoming spaces for teens who may not have many other safe, supervised places to go after school. My background in reader’s advisory, early literacy support, and digital literacy training would allow me to contribute meaningfully from day one.
statement_of_interest.explain_why_considered_employment_line_4: On a personal level, returning to work in my hometown library feels like a chance to give back to the community that invested in me. I understand the local context—seasonal work patterns, tourism, the mix of long-time residents and newcomers—and I genuinely care about making the library a central, trusted hub for learning and connection. I am comfortable working evenings and weekends, collaborating across departments, and pitching in wherever needed, whether that is shelving, setting up for programs, or helping a patron print a resume five minutes before closing. I see this position not just as a job, but as a long-term commitment to helping Sandusky Library remain a vibrant, welcoming, and forward-looking resource for everyone who walks through its doors.
